about.title: about me
about.text: I'm a creative director & visual strategist studying Cognitive Science and Business at UC San Diego. Currently building: this site, my javascript proficiency, a newfound relationship w/ acrylic paint. Check out my portfolio and resume for more

items.title: Triton Trading Group (2025-26)
items.summary: Brand and recruitment design for Triton Trading Group — UCSD’s fastest-growing pre-professional business organization.
items.description: Triton Trading Group (TTG) is UCSD’s fastest-growing pre-professional business organization. This work spans social recaps, portfolio headers, Spring 2026 recruitment, and a Winter 2025 tabling banner — all built to feel cohesive across campus touchpoints and digital channels.

sections.title: Bimonthly quant recap
sections.description: Recap graphics for LinkedIn and Instagram, giving followers a snapshot of what TTG’s quant team has been working on.

items.description: Mid-month recap covering new analysis, tools, and team updates.

items.description: Late-May recap closing out the current quant cycle.

items.title: Scotscoop Logo (2023)
items.summary: Logo design for Scot Scoop News, the student news publication at Carlmont High School.
items.description: Identity work for Scot Scoop News — from early mascot explorations through a final editorial mark used across the publication's digital and print presence.

caseStudy.note: Final double-S monogram, inspired by the New York Times letterform tradition. Hand-drawn first, then refined in Adobe Illustrator for the site and social channels.

notes: Early explorations built on Scot Scoop’s existing identity—light, pun-driven directions around ice cream, broadcast, and the double meaning of “scoop.”
notesAfter: Mascot-style S marks and companion logos for ScotCenter, the publication’s broadcast channel.

caseStudy.text: The direction shifted toward something more editorial—credible, restrained, and built to represent serious student journalism.

columns.note: Vector lockup deployed across Scot Scoop’s site, social profiles, and print collateral.
